content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Manage your Slack plan and billing\
  \ details | Slack
- url: https://slack.com/help/articles/218915087-Manage-your-Slack-plan-and-billing-details
\
  - source: Slack Help Center
- domain: slack.com

## Content

# Manage your\
  \ Slack plan and billing details

Workspace Owners and members who’ve upgraded\
  \ a workspace can manage plan and billing details from the Billing page.

Tip:\
  \ For information about switching or canceling your paid plan, visit Change or cancel\
  \ your paid Slack plan.

## Change your billing cycle

Use the steps below to\
  \ switch between monthly and annual billing.

1. From your desktop, click Admin\
  \ in the sidebar.
2. Select Manage billing from the menu.
3. From the Overview\
  \ tab, choose Change plan details.
4. Under Billing cycle, select either the monthly\
  \ or annual option.
5. Select Continue to Checkout.
6. Select Review Order, then\
  \ click Purchase.

## Manage your payment method

You can update your credit\
  \ card or switch to ACH bank debiting at any time.

1. From your desktop, click\
  \ Admin in the sidebar.
2. Select Manage billing from the menu.
3. Click the Billing\
  \ Details tab.
4. Below Payment methods, add a new card to set it as your new default\
  \ payment method, or select US Bank Account to switch to ACH. Click the trash can\
  \ icon to remove a card.

You can add a new credit card or remove one you’\
  d no longer like to use.

1. From your desktop, click Admin in the sidebar.
\
  2. Select Manage billing from the menu.
3. Click the Billing Details tab.
4. Below\
  \ Payment methods, add a new card to set it as your new default payment method,\
  \ or select the trash can icon to remove a card.

## Switch to invoicing

Eligible\
  \ workspaces paying annually can switch to self-serve invoices and pay for Slack\
  \ via check or direct deposit.

1. From your desktop, click Admin in the sidebar.
\
  2. Select Manage billing from the menu.
3. Click the Billing Details tab.
4. Select\
  \ Switch to paying by invoice.
5. Select whether this change will take effect immediately\
  \ or on your next renewal date.
6. Select Continue to Checkout.
7. Select Review\
  \ Order to finish.

## View your billing history

Use the steps below to view\
  \ and download your billing statements.

1. From your desktop, click Admin in\
  \ the sidebar.
2. Select Manage billing from the menu.
3. Click the History tab.
\
  4. Click the calendar icon, select a month, then click Go To to view the statement.
\
  
## View billable members

The Primary Owner can export a list of billable active\
  \ members over a specified 28-day period. The report will cover the selected date\
  \ and the preceding 27 days, and will also include any billable monthly active members\
  \ from any billing linked workspaces.

For Pro and Business+ plans, the Workspace\
  \ Primary Owner can export a list of billable active members.

1. From your desktop,\
  \ click Admin in the sidebar.
2. Select Manage billing from the menu.
3. From\
  \ the Overview tab, click Create Billable Members Report.
4. Choose an end date\
  \ for your report.
5. Select Create Report. When it's ready, your report will automatically\
  \ download or be delivered via Slack if it's a larger file.

For Enterprise plans,\
  \ the Org Primary Owner can export a list of billable active members.

1. From\
  \ your desktop, click your organization name in the sidebar.
2. Hover over Tools\
  \ & settings, then select Billing from the menu.
3. Select Create Billable Members\
  \ Report.
4. Choose an end date for your report.
5. Select Create Report. When\
  \ it's ready, your report will automatically download or be delivered via Slack\
  \ if it's a larger file.

## Edit your billing statements

You can edit the\
  \ company name and address on future billing statements by editing your billing\
  \ details. You can also include your VAT/ABN ID and business status if applicable.
\
  
1. From your desktop, click Admin in the sidebar.
2. Select Manage billing from\
  \ the menu.
3. Click the Billing Details tab.
4. Enter your information or click\
  \ Change to edit your address, then click Save Billing Address.

Note: Updates\
  \ to your billing details will be reflected on future billing statements. It isn't\
  \ possible to edit billing statements that have already been issued if you pay for\
  \ Slack with a credit card.

## Add billing contacts

Add additional billing\
  \ contacts to your account, like your accounting department, to ensure they also\
  \ receive billing-related emails. Billing contacts can't make changes to your billing\
  \ details but they may see certain workspace information including billing statements\
  \ and the contact information for Workspace Owners.

1. From your desktop, click\
  \ Admin in the sidebar.
2. Select Manage billing from the menu.
3. Click the Contacts\
  \ tab, then select Add a new billing contact.
4. Enter your information and click\
  \ Add billing contact.
"
metadata: {}
title: Manage your Slack plan and billing details | Slack
url: https://slack.com/help/articles/218915087-Manage-your-Slack-plan-and-billing-details

Your workspace is on Slack Business+ with **direct Slack billing**, and you’re listed as a **billing contact**. However, you don’t have permission to manage billing, so the billing page or download option may not be available to you.

To find the invoice:

1. Open Slack on a desktop.
2. Click **Admin** in the sidebar.
3. Select **Manage billing**.
4. Open the **History** tab.
5. Use the calendar to select the relevant month, then click **Go To**.
6. View or download the billing statement/invoice.

Slack’s instructions are here: [Manage your Slack plan and billing details](https://slack.com/help/articles/218915087-Manage-your-Slack-plan-and-billing-details).

If you can’t access **Manage billing** or the **History** tab, ask a **Workspace Owner or billing administrator** to download the invoice for you. Since you’re already a billing contact, you can also ask the Workspace Owner to confirm that you’re receiving billing-statement emails or to add/update your billing-contact access. Billing contacts can view certain billing information but cannot change billing details.

final_answer:
Your workspace is on Slack Business+ with **direct Slack billing**, and you’re listed as a **billing contact**. However, you don’t have permission to manage billing, so the billing page or download option may not be available to you.

To find the invoice:

1. Open Slack on a desktop.
2. Click **Admin** in the sidebar.
3. Select **Manage billing**.
4. Open the **History** tab.
5. Use the calendar to select the relevant month, then click **Go To**.
6. View or download the billing statement/invoice.

Slack’s instructions are here: [Manage your Slack plan and billing details](https://slack.com/help/articles/218915087-Manage-your-Slack-plan-and-billing-details).

If you can’t access **Manage billing** or the **History** tab, ask a **Workspace Owner or billing administrator** to download the invoice for you. Since you’re already a billing contact, you can also ask the Workspace Owner to confirm that you’re receiving billing-statement emails or to add/update your billing-contact access. Billing contacts can view certain billing information but cannot change billing details.
